{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

[https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

[https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

[https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

(1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[:en:Shijō Street|Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[:en:Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q710302]] Wikidata name: Matsunoo Taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[:en:Shijō Street|Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[:en:Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[sn:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[sn:atsunoo Taisha]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q710302}}

It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. (1959). Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. [[sn:commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q710302]] Wikidata name: Matsunoo Taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[:en:Shijō Street|Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[:en:Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] {{wikidata link|Q710302}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] {{wikidata link|Q710302}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q710302]] Wikidata name: Matsunoo Taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[:en:Shijō Street|Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[:en:Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] {{wikidata link|Q710302}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q710302]] Wikidata name: Matsunoo Taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[:en:Shijō Street|Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[:en:Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Taisha]] }} [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] {{wikidata link|Q710302}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] [[commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Matsunoo-taisha]][[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]][[en:Matsunoo Taisha]][[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]][[it:Santuario Matsunoo]][[ja:松尾大社]][[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]][[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]][[zh:松尾大社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q710302]] Wikidata name: Matsunoo Taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan}} {{infobox religious building | name = Matsunoo-taisha
松尾大社 | image = Matsuo Taisha honden2.jpg | caption = The ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', at Matsunoo-taisha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|00|01|N|135|41|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ō-yamagui-no-kami]]
[[:en:Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto|Nakatsu-shima-hime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] | founded_by = | established = 701 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Arashiyamamiya-chō, [[:en:Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto|Nishikyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 616-0024 | website = {{URL|http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Taisha'''|松尾大社|extra=''Matsunoo Taisha/ Matsuo Taisha''}}, formerly {{Nihongo|'''Matsunoo Jinja'''|松尾神社}}, is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the far western end of [[:en:Shijō Street|Shijō Street]], approximately 1.3 kilometers south of the [[:en:Arashiyama|Arashiyama]] district of [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. It is home to a [[:en:Spring (hydrosphere)|spring]] at the base of the mountain, Arashiyama, that is believed to be blessed. It is said that during the move of the capital from [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] to Kyoto, a noble saw a turtle bathing under the spring's waterfall and created a shrine there. It is one of the oldest shrines in the Kyoto area, its founding extending back to 700 CE. The restorative properties of the spring bring many local [[sake]] and [[:en:Miso|Miso]] companies to the shrine for prayers that their product will be blessed. The shrine also serves a ''kinpaku miki'' (gold leaf filled blessed sake) during ''[[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]]''. ==History== The shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74–75.] In 965, [[:en:Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami]] ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''[[kami]]'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Matsunoo Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116–117. From 1871 through 1946, Matsunoo-taisha was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. == Shofuen Gardens == After a new set of buildings was finished in 1973, modernist garden designer [[:en:Mirei Shigemori|Mirei Shigemori]] was brought in to design new garden areas at Matsuo Taisha. Shigemori designed three new garden areas, which were completed in 1975: * Kyokosui no Niwa 曲水の庭 (Garden of the Winding Stream) with a clear flowing stream in the style of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] * Jōko no Niwa 上古の庭 (Prehistoric Garden), a setting of large stones that references the ancient Mt. Matsuo shrine stone behind the shrine buildings * Horai no Niwa 蓬莱の庭 (Garden of Horai), a garden referencing the land of [[:en:Mount Penglai|Horai]] in Chinese and Japanese myth The gardens at Matsuo Taisha were Shigemori's last project; his son, Kanto, supervised the final work after Shigemori's death on March 12, 1975.{{Cite book |last=Tschumi |first=Christian |title=Mirei Shigemori: Modernizing the Japanese Garden |publisher=Stone Bridge Press |year=2005 |edition=1st |pages=108–115}} ==images== File:Matsuo Taisha front gate.jpg|Front gate File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan01s3.jpg|Horai Garden of Shofuen File:Shofuen Matsuo-taisha Kyoto Japan07s3.jpg|Kyokusui Garden of Shofuen File:Tsukuyomi shrine Kyoto.jpg|Shrine to [[Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|Tsukuyomi]] ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Twenty-Two Shrines|Twenty-Two Shrines]] *[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|399449}} * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} ==External links== *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/en/ Official Site] {{in lang|en}} *[http://www.matsunoo.or.jp/ Official Site] {{in lang|ja}} {{Sannō Ichijitsu Shintō}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Matsunoo Taisha]] {{Commons category|Matsunoo-taisha}} [[de:Matsunoo-Schrein]] [[fr:Matsunoo-taisha]] [[it:Santuario Matsunoo]] [[ja:松尾大社]] [[my:မဆွတ်နိုအိုတိုင်းရှ]] [[ru:Мацуноо-тайся]] [[zh:松尾大社]] {{wikidata link|Q710302}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 8th century]] [[Category:Gardens in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
